quote: This book is about the basic structural composition of written series pitches, series proposals and series bibles.
the concepts presented here are the same for any dramatic show, sci-fi or otherwise.
the terms ‘series pitch’, ‘series proposal’ and ‘series bible’ may be used in different ways by others in film and television.
This book is not the begin-all end-all of series pitches, series proposals and series bibles. The subject is as widely varied as there are ways to write them.
Even though each position had different responsibilities, the focus of all of them was always on good, concise, gripping story.
As the television industry developed, it became more and more important for all storytellers, writers and producers alike, to ‘be on the same page’, immediately and consistently.
We needed not just a benchmark for each show, but a starting point, a virtual lighthouse in the fog of unintended ‘artistic wander’, that often happens with the creative process.
As the industry developed, that benchmark, that lighthouse, became the television series bible.

Originally the series bible was an afterthought, a way of staying consistent with changing staff. It eventually became a way to lock down the starting point, the vision of what the series would become.
Later, it became a development tool, and eventually, a type of pitch tool, in its more abbreviated form, the TV series proposal.
It is said that television is in a new golden age, but we submit that our entire entertainment culture is in a new golden age.
with the advent of e-book series, web series, streaming series, graphic novel series, et al, the use of a series bible is more pertinent than ever before.
Many believe that the modern series started with the 18th century’s Three Musketeers and Sherlock Holmes.

the series as we know it really began with pulp fiction. Pulp fiction was a phenomenon of inexpensive short novel printed on cheap pulp paper. These books hit the publishing scene around 1896 through the 50’s. Pulp fiction then led to radio drama serials, that in turn led to, yes, series television.
Series development is the development of a long form story from a concept, into a working series of ‘episodes’.

Traditionally, series development begins with coming up with a compelling concept, a good ‘what if’ for a show, then moves forward to: -The Series Arc: The mystery or quest…the story engine that propels and sustains the series over the length of the show.
The Episode Arc. The incidents that propels each episode. The pattern or patterns that a given episode's story could have. An episode mini-quest or mystery that propels its story engine.
Genre: What type of story is being told. Is it police procedural, sci-fi, romantic comedy, etc. Or some mix of multiple genres?
Tone, Theme, and Style: Is it a gritty urban drama, showing us how to fix a broken city, or is it a light-hearted urban comedy that shows us the lighter side of poverty?
Character Development: Who the characters are, what they want, what they unknowingly need and what they do about their situation? (This very importantly includes villains!)
Storylines: All the possible directions and ways that make sense for the show to move storytelling forward. This should lead to several episode concepts for your bible or proposal.
World building: What the setting is, what the pertinent backstory is of the world, how it works and how these all affect the characters.
Set Pieces: What are the locations of the series? Are they police stations, space ships, apartments or cruise ships? Then make a glossary of them for your series.

Before you begin developing your series, past the initial concept, you should ask yourself several key questions. These questions will test your concept for both ‘audience’ interest and most especially, for your own.
if you don’t have enough interest in your series, you won’t write it. If you don’t write that first pilot script/novel/webcomic, et al, you won’t have the staying power your series needs.
if you don’t ask the hard questions about why your audience will love the series, they won’t spend the time on your first episode, much less the rest of the series.
We suggest that there are always three, the main series hook, a multi-episode hook and a hook for each episode
Series can have similarities, but they can’t be carbon copies or so similar to an existing series that there is nothing new to explore in your ‘world’.
A series pitch is short and sweet, a series proposal should be longer, and a full series bible…well, that can be as long as needed. Series ready bibles have been know to hit 100 plus pages in length.

A series pitch can be verbal, or written. A verbal pitch is often called an ‘elevator pitch’. This is a pitch that is short, concise and with enough punch to convey your concept, and hopefully get the sale.
Written series pitches are usually one to five pages in length, preferably no longer. The good ones usually have a great elevator pitch/logline as its first few sentences.

A written pitch elaborates enough in a few pages to give enough of a sense of the series, without being so in depth that a Producer won’t take the time to read it. Anything longer, and you are taking a chance it won’t be read at all.
Like a pitch, a series proposal can be verbal, or written. We are focusing on the written proposal here.
Written series proposals are usually 6 to 15 pages in length, preferably no longer.

A written proposal is usually provided when an interested Producer wants more details after a successful pitch. It gives a much more detailed look at the series for them to make a better decision. It can also prove to be a good development tool for any writer developing his or her series.
Unlike a series pitch or series proposal, a series bible is created for or during a series production.
They are usually more detailed and are usually 50 to 100 plus pages in length. (Note: Production Series Bibles are often double spaced or at 1.5)

For many, this is not just a guide, but the ultimate development tool for a series. However, it is good to note that when writing a series, its direction may veer in unexpected and better directions than the bible.

Note for this section: In this section, a short four plus page episode summary is usually provided for proposals. This summary should not be taken as a definitive template for all stories, it is not. This is merely an example of one type of story and what it’s type of structure entails.

There has always been unspoken information on how to accomplish specific tasks for writing. Unfortunately these tasks have been concealed, on purpose or not, by the publishers, studios, and companies that use them as industry “trade secrets”.

What follows is a sample series pitch. We need to remind you, there are so many formats out there, that someone is bound to disagree with this format. That’s fine. Again, we are simply sharing what has worked for our company and our clients over the years.
CHARACTER TITLE NAME IN CAPS, age, how the character appears and years of duty/job.
Character’s personal problem(s) and how he spends his time. Demeanor and how it does or doesn’t change, depending on the situation.
What he/she thinks of the current situation, how his personal history affects that view and what emotional gut instinct is about his opinion on the current situation.
How he tests others and/or their resolve to prove themselves. How the character will evolve over the course of the series and what traits stay static.
What type of hero and accompanying characters, the inciting incident of the story, the internal dramatic conflict of the series, the external dramatic conflict of the series, the quest of the series.
What type of hero, the discovery/impetus that leads to the inciting incident, the inciting incident, the ironic dramatic push of the series and the series quest
Name of character, Type of character, main emotional push, ironic push of the series, Internal struggle due to the ironic push
Character name, relationship to the series quest/mystery, job during the series, the dramatic secret that he/she keeps from the other characters that pertain to the quest/mystery
Character name, type of character, hint of a shadowy past. Establish that the past is threatening to be revealed by a new, threatening character
Character name, type of character, reason for his/her station in life, hidden/secret plot that can affect the series mystery/quest
Character name, type of character, station in life, type of person, relationship to malicious character and how he/she helps that character

Character name, type of character, character past, connection with the main character, what supporting character he/she confides in, secret he/she keeps that can affect relationship with the main character.
Character name, station in life, his/her relation to a supporting character and their designs, character’s abilities that can affect those designs and the series quest success/failure
Establish the dramatic and ironic conditions that the characters, the tensions this causes and the external threats that the mystery/quest brings them
Description of the range of series stories locations, description of the three to four dramatic focal points of the series, ending with the primary mystery/quest

Character name, character description, station in life, any personal internal loss, how that loss/pain has affected the character, how that led to the inciting incident, how the inciting incident led to the dramatic irony of the series, and the series mystery and/or quest

Character name, station in life, past station in life. Mutual past with main character, current relationship with the main character, how/why they work together. What the main character thinks of him/her, what relationship he/she has with a different character that could/or does impact his/her relationship with the main character

The character’s name, how he or she fits in the team/show, What he/she thinks of the main character, What the character’s main goal is/was. How does the character feel now, after the ‘set sail’ incident? What special relationship he/she has with another character, that could cause problems. What is the character’s special gift that makes him/her valuable? What is the dramatic ‘Rock and a hard place’ decision he/she must make?

Character’s name, visual aesthetic and uniqueness amongst the team/crew? How and why he/she doesn’t fit in. How did the character become who they are now? How does their current predicament affect their mindset now? What is the character’s ‘rock and a hard place’ dramatic choice?

Character’s name, rank and/or position, what he/she did/does that pertains to the central mystery and/or quest. What his/her purpose is now. What secret and/or agenda the character holds that can affect the other characters. How does this secret and/or agenda weigh on the character?

Character’s name, career and the original purpose for being in his position. How his past connects with the central mystery and/or quest and what he/she holds back. What his/her new hidden purpose is and how that connects with the central mystery and/or quest. How he/she acts to control others to that purpose? What unexpected thing results from that strategy that puts this plan in jeopardy?

The Character’s name, rank/position? The character’s key personality traits and how a new character affects those traits? How he/she first reacts to that character and how that reaction changes? How she helps the new character with his/her plans and/or ulterior motives? What other character he/she engages that makes his/her new relationship more complicated? How is he/she coerced by a character to use/abuse his/her relationship with a key character?

Establishes the Current World of the Series: When, Who, Where & Why of the story. The Why should be the physical motivation quest and the emotional quest, at the beginning. The Catalyst moment that teases and creates the first dramatic question.
The answer to the first dramatic question is one or more related surprise(s). Usually, involves conflict and/or action and/or imminent conflict and/or action.
The dramatic truth of the surprise(s) fully erupt in conflict and/or action. One or more earlier foreshadowed dramatic surprise(s) engages dramatically.
Characters become aware of the dramatic event know as the Inciting Incident, which is defined as an event or decision that begins a story’s problem. The character(s) realize what has now occurred.
Conflict and/or Action begins due to the Inciting Incident. Alliances are decided or decision(s) to embark on a physical journey is/are decided
Another action or event has occurred that compounds the effect of the Inciting Incident.
Characters take stock of what has happened.
Tensely, Characters strategize on how to recuperate from the Inciting Incident.
Characters tensely interact and initiate the layout of the plan of action to recuperate from the Inciting Incident.
Plan of action is tensely agreed to, but not all characters agree or are happy about the decision and/or direction.
The high stakes that have resulted from the Inciting Incident (Ex.endangered person(s), item(s), etc.> is in dire jeopardy.)
Character’s plan to lick their wounds and/or seek ways to find the jeopardized person(s), item(s) or situation(s))
Characters come up against conflict with each other while licking their wounds. Can and should involve subtle or over subterfuge.
Against the odds, wounds have been sufficiently fixed, and the jeopardized person(s) and/or item(s) locations has been found/discovered. Now the character(s) are underway to retrieve the jeopardized.
The situation and stakes to obtain, fix and/or rescue the jeopardized are established and a rough plan is hatched.
More tension(s)/problem(s) is/are revealed in planning the undertaking to obtain, fix and/or rescue the jeopardized.

Character(s) practice and/or move for/to the undertaking and tension(s) run high. This is usually due to either overwhelmingly bad odds, internal personal strife and/or inter character strife…or all of the above.
Characters arrive at the Undertaking, resolute but worried.
First part of the Undertaking plan begins.
The Undertaking brings conflict and/or action. During this sequence, a surprise reveals a character’s hard decision…selfishness or selflessness for another character and/or cause.
Another character finds an unforeseen wrinkle in the plan. He/she/they must adjust and take action.
The character with the surprise hard decision between selfishness and selflessness makes a decision, that may be uncharacteristic for him/her.

The adjusted plan reveals more complications and a character questions his or others’ ability and/or motives. The complication sets actions in motion that establish the main dramatic quest for the entire series.
The undertaking seems to be working, if imperfectly and/or with losses.
The Undertaking and/or one of its main characters is in jeopardy. The Undertaking is successful, but at a cost of either life, limb, resources, or a combination thereof.
The character(s) have succeeded and leave/retreat from the undertaking.
The characters embrace their successes, alone and/or with each other.
Secondary characters ponder and/or show the results of their actions and/or successes.
Character(s) confront each other over their actions, or analyze the new reality their success has brought.
The Epilogue point that reveals the mystery of the series antagonist.

What is the episode’s problem/teaser? What predicament does the problem/teaser place the characters in? What must the characters do to fix their predicament, against overwhelming odds? What secondary problem/danger is discovered if they don’t fix their situation soon? (ticking clock) What personal problems must one or more characters overcome to succeed?

What catalyst has occurred over a given amount of time that has led to the current ‘world’? What conflict(s) has this world created due to the catalyst? How do the authorities in this new world act due to the catalyst? How does the populace in this new world act due to the catalyst and the authorities?

What is the event that initiates the series, that bridges between the catalyst for the existing world and inciting incident? What leads to the inciting incident and how does the series’ “set sail” moment occur? What is the new predicament that leads the characters to their new world? How does this create/expose the central mystery and/or series quest?
How must the characters must/or agree to work together? Is it a tense détente, or tense working relationship? Who is happy with the arrangement? Who is not? Why?
Physical description, age, position of the main protagonist
Personal background of the main protagonist’s family? Original plans for the future? What led to his/her current position?
How the protagonist is seen/perceived by his/her peers. What are the attributes and/or features of his/her character?

What key attribute forms his/her decision-making standards? What has occurred in the past that has or could change that standard? How has that past occurrence changed the protagonist and his actions and bearing?

How does the protagonist’s past affect how he/she feels about the antagonist group? How does the protagonist deal with the antagonist on a daily basis? How and why does the protagonist deal with the antagonist group? How does this dynamic affect the protagonist’s psyche?
How does the protagonist treat the antagonists? His Team? How do his/her feelings change towards the antagonists? His team?

How do his/her feelings towards the antagonists change during the series? Generally, what things will the protagonist discover during the course of the series about; The central mystery, the central quest, his team, his antagonists?

The recurring character’s personal background. His/her cultural background and how that connects with the main protagonists and/or antagonists? What kind of person is the character at heart? What motivates the character in contrast with his/her background?

How does the character and the main protagonist deal with each other, and why? How does the main protagonist feel about the character, and why? What other character plays into their interplay? How do these characters intersect with the central mystery and/or central quest?

The ethnicity, position, age and physical ability of the recurring character? What are the personality markers of the character? How does his/her personality affect dealings with the main antagonists? How does his/her presence affect one or more key characters? What main personality quirk is he/she know for?
What is the age of the character, quality of his/her personal relationships?
What is the character’s relationship with the main protagonist and how/where did it come about? How does the main protagonist rely on the character, and how frequently?

What other character most relates to the character? What mannerism(s) most attracts/detracts the other character from him/her? How does their relationship affect other characters/antagonist/protagonist, relations?

How did the character’s personality affect their early professional years? What occurred that led the character to his/her current position/situation? What key moment led to his/her current place in time?
What cultural traditions does the character observe or ignore, and why? How does he/she deal with this, and why?
What type of leadership skills does the character have or not? How do other characters respond to these skills or lack thereof?

How does the character react to situations in or out of her control? What is the character’s personal background and how was he/she raised? How did this background lead to her position before the series springboard event?
How does this background make him/her key in addressing the series central mystery and/or central quest? What general secrets does he/she have that relates to the mystery and/or quest?
What is a one sentence description of the character’s way through life and how they have handled the hand they were dealt?

What is/are the character’s trade/positions held? What is the character’s position with the current protagonists and/or antagonists? What has or is the character planning that is or might be contrary to the status quo?
After the advent of the series springboard event, how did this affect his/her psyche and growth?
How did this growth contrast with his/her past? How does this contrast make him/her feel in their current state?
What does the character do on a daily basis to deal with his/her current predicament?
How do others view this character? How do others assist and/or hinder this character?
Character’s ethnic background and how it affects his/her demeanor? What character does he/she connect with and/or affects his/her outlook on a personal level and why?
How has the character’s demeanor been shaped by childhood and why? Where did he/she spend their childhood? How did that affect his/her childhood and why?
How did she come to be in his/her current situation, and how does it complement his/her demeanor? How did his/her new position reveal his/her personal strengths or weaknesses?
How did the series springboard even affect his/her personal challenges? What character(s) help or hinder the characters ability to deal or not?
How is this character seen by others of the opposite sex and what character does he/she find attractive, but problematic?
Description of the main antagonist group? How the antagonist group connects/corresponds to the protagonist group? Attributes and features of the main antagonist group?
Description of the main series setpiece/location
First part of the Undertaking plan begins.
The Undertaking brings conflict and/or action. During this sequence, a surprise reveals a character's hard decision...selfishness or selflessness for another character and/or cause.
Another character finds an unforeseen wrinkle in the plan. He/she/they must adjust and take action.
The character with the surprise hard decision between selfishness and selflessness makes a decision, that may be uncharacteristic for him/her.

The adjusted plan reveals more complications and a character questions his or others' ability and/or motives. The complication sets actions in motion that establish the main dramatic quest for the entire series.
The undertaking seems to be working, if imperfectly and/or with losses.
The Undertaking and/or one of its main characters is in jeopardy. The Undertaking is successful, but at a cost of either life, limb, resources, or a combination thereof.
The character(s) have succeeded and leave/retreat from the undertaking.
The characters embrace their successes, alone and/or with each other.
Secondary characters ponder and/or show the results of their actions and/or successes.
Character(s) confront each other over their actions, or analyze the new reality their success has brought.
The Epilogue point that reveals the mystery of the series antagonist.

What is the episode's problem/teaser? What predicament does the problem/teaser place the characters in? What must the characters do to fix their predicament, against overwhelming odds? What secondary problem/danger is discovered if they don't fix their situation soon? (ticking clock) What personal problems must one or more characters overcome to succeed?

What is the episode's problem/teaser? What personal problems must one or more characters overcome to succeed? What secondary problem/danger occurs/is discovered? What mysterious puzzle piece must be deciphered, quickly (ticking clock) before their problem/danger becomes permanent?

What is the benign beginning purpose of the characters? What is discovered that seems/is connected to the central mystery/quest? How or what does the discovery do to threaten the characters? What secondary danger has made fixing the situation with the discovery, time sensitive? (Ticking clock)

How does the navigating the central mystery/quest reveal a clue/set piece at a new location? How does that new set piece put them in immediate danger? How does that set piece put them at odds with a new threat that wants to retrieve the set piece from them? What makes it impossible for them to give a new threat what they want, in the time that they want? (ticking clock) What are three things they must accomplish before an established threat to their survival occurs?
Unlike a series pitch or series proposal, a series bible is created for or during a series production.
They are usually more detailed and are usually 50 to 100 plus pages in length. (Note: Production Series Bibles are often double spaced or at 1.5)

For many, this is not just a guide, but the ultimate development tool for a series. However, it is good to note that when writing a series, its direction may veer in unexpected and better directions than the bible.
What is a single sentence explanation/description of the series.

What catalyst has occurred over a given amount of time that has led to the current 'world'? What conflict(s) has this world created due to the catalyst? How do the authorities in this new world act due to the catalyst? How does the populace in this world act due to the catalyst and the authorities?

What is the event that initiates the series, that bridges between the catalyst for the existing world and inciting incident? What leads to the inciting incident and how does the series' "set sail" moment occur? What is the new predicament that leads the characters to their new world? How does this create/expose the central mystery and/or series quest?
How must the characters must/or agree to work together? Is it a tense detente, or tense working relationship? Who is happy with the arrangement? Who is not? Why?
Description of the main series setpiece/location
Description of the main antagonist group? How the antagonist group connects/corresponds to the protagonist group? Attributes and features of the main antagonist group?
Physical description, age, position of the main protagonist
Personal background of the main protagonist's family? Original plans for the future? What led to his/her current position?
How the protagonist is seen/perceived by his/her peers. What are the attributes and/or features of his/her character?

What key attribute forms his/her decision-making standards? What has occurred in the past that has or could change that standard? How has that past occurrence changed the protagonist and his actions and bearing?

How does the protagonist's past affect how he/she feels about the antagonist group? How does the protagonist deal with the antagonist on a daily basis? How and why does the protagonist deal with the antagonist group? How does this dynamic affect the protagonist's psyche?
How does the protagonist treat the antagonists? His Team? How do his/her feelings change towards the antagonists? His team?

How do his/her feelings towards the antagonists change during the series? Generally, what things will the protagonist discover during the course of the series about; The central mystery, the central quest, his team, his antagonists?

The recurring character's personal background. His/her cultural background and how that connects with the main protagonists and/or antagonists? What kind of person is the character at heart? What motivates the character in contrast with his/her background?

How did the character's personality affect their early professional years? What occurred that led the character to his/her current position/situation? What key moment led to his/her current place in time?
What cultural traditions does the character observe or ignore, and why? How does he/she deal with this, and why?
What type of leadership skills does the character have or not? How do other characters respond to these skills or lack thereof?

How does the character and the main protagonist deal with each other, and why? How does the main protagonist feel about the character, and why? What other character plays into their interplay? How do these characters intersect with the central mystery and/or central quest?

The ethnicity, position, age and physical ability of the recurring character? What are the personality markers of the character? How does his/her personality affect dealings with the main antagonists? How does his/her presence affect one or more key characters? What main personality quirk is he/she know for?
What is the age of the character, quality of his/her personal relationships?
What is the character's relationship with the main protagonist and how/where did it come about? How does the main protagonist rely on the character, and how frequently?

What other character most relates to the character? What mannerism(s) most attracts/detracts the other character from him/her? How does their relationship affect other characters/antagonist/protagonist, relations?
What kind of background was the character raised in? What expectations were placed on the character by him/herself and/or others? What motivated him/her to achieve his/her current position?
Name, position, ethnic background and personal position before the series springboard incident? How his closest personal relations felt towards him before the series springboard incident?
After the advent of the series springboard event, how did this affect his/her psyche and growth?
How did this growth contrast with his/her past? How does this contrast make him/her feel in their current state?
What does the character do on a daily basis to deal with his/her current predicament?
How do others view this character? How do others assist and/or hinder this character?
Character's ethnic background and how it affects his/her demeanor? What character does he/she connect with and/or affects his/her outlook on a personal level and why?
Character's name, position, demeanor, ability...and personal demon and/or struggle?
How has the character's demeanor been shaped by childhood and why? Where did he/she spend their childhood? How did that affect his/her childhood and why?
How did the beginning of his/her professional life change him/her? What struggles did the character have and why? What was his/her first professional job and was it smooth or rocky and why?
How did she come to be in his/her current situation, and how does it complement his/her demeanor? How did his/her new position reveal his/her personal strengths or weaknesses?
How did the series springboard even affect his/her personal challenges? What character(s) help or hinder the characters ability to deal or not?
How is this character seen by others of the opposite sex and what character does he/she find attractive, but problematic?
Name, rank and nickname of character? His/her background to moving up in the ranks, and its pitfalls? Why his/her position during the series springboard event was key and/or monumental?

How does the character react to situations in or out of her control? What is the character's personal background and how was he/she raised? How did this background lead to her position before the series springboard event? How does this background make him/her key in addressing the series central mystery and/or central quest? What general secrets does he/she have that relates to the mystery and/or quest?

Name of ne'er do well character and his/her position? What is his/her reason(s) for being in their position? What is the most accurate assessment of the character's negative traits and how others perceive those traits?
What is a one sentence description of the character's way through life and how they have handled the hand they were dealt?

What is/are the character's trade/positions held? What is the character's position with the current protagonists and/or antagonists? What has or is the character planning that is or might be contrary to the status quo?

The Character's name, rank/position? The character's key personality traits and how a new character affects those traits? How he/she first reacts to that character and how that reaction changes? How she helps the new character with his/her plans and/or ulterior motives? What other character he/she engages that makes his/her new relationship more complicated? How is he/she coerced by a character to use/abuse his/her relationship with a key character?
There has always been unspoken information on how to accomplish specific tasks for writing.
Unfortunately these tasks have been concealed, on purpose or not, by the publishers, studios, and companies that use them as industry "trade secrets".
